title: helianthemum vulgare subsp. scopolii
intro: Helianthemum vulgare subsp. scopolii, also known as Rockrose or Snow Rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is a perennial herb that grows in rocky and dry areas, producing white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Rockrose is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to relieve stress, promote relaxation, and support overall health.
